items.title: Peel police identify 17-year-old boys as suspects in fatal Brampton shooting
items.summary: Peel police have identified two teenagers as suspects in a fatal shooting in Brampton.

items.title: Red-level tornado warning issued for Halton, Peel regions: Environment Canada
items.summary: Environment Canada said it is tracking a severe thunderstorm just east of Milton that could possibly produce a tornado and move through Oakville and Mississauga.

items.title: Severe Thunderstorm and Tornado Watch issued for Mississauga; damaging winds and heavy rain possible today
items.summary: Residents should take cover immediately if threatening weather approaches and continue to monitor weather alerts and local media for updates. Storm conditions are expected to continue through the afternoon across much of southern Ontario. Given the localized nature of thunderstorms and tornadoes, exact impacts and locations can be difficult to predict until storms develop and move into the area. Storm conditions are expected to begin around noon today, with the most significant impacts anticipated through the early afternoon.

items.title: City outdoor pools and recreation programs resume normal operations as air quality continues to improve
items.summary: Due to wildfires in other parts of Ontario, Mississauga experienced poor air quality and a Very High (10+) Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) last week. As a result, to support public health and safety, outdoor pools, and Mississauga Celebration Square wading pool were closed and some recreation programs were adjusted. Based on reports from Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC), air quality is expected to continue improving through the day.
